summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering routine business including bill payments, approval of out-of-town travel for fire department personnel to inspect a new quint apparatus in Florida, and scheduling a special meeting for a zoning text amendment public hearing. The board also objected to proposed utility rate increases from Duke Energy, accepted donations, and accepted a firefighter's resignation following an executive session.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (May 27 – June 9, 2026) – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Deputy Chief Lynch, Captain Rose, and Captain Estep to E-ONE in Ocala, FL (June 24-26, 2026) for quint final inspection – passed
Motion to approve Goshen Lions Club use of township property for Flags for Heroes display in July and November 2026 – passed
Motion to approve letter to PUCO objecting to proposed distribution rate increases (natural gas) – passed
Motion to accept donation of food gift cards from CP AAA to the administration department – passed
Motion to schedule a special Board of Trustees meeting on June 22, 2026 at 6 p.m. for public hearing on case ZC2026-005 (M3 text amendment) – passed
Motion to enter executive session per ORC 121.22(G)(1) at 6:14 p.m. – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter/EMT Ryan DeAngelis effective June 5, 2026 – passed
Motion to accept $500 donation to the fire department from an anonymous donor – passed
Motion to adjourn at 6:58 p.m. – passed
action_items: Chief Rose to send PUCO objection letter on behalf of the Board regarding natural gas rate increases
Deputy Chief Lynch, Captain Rose, and Captain Estep to travel to E-ONE in Ocala, FL for quint final inspection (June 24-26)
Chad to rent a skid steer attachment for roadside mowing until the new side mower arrives July 5th
Chad/Administration to continue working with Donna Rosick on OPWC grant for paving Snyder, Smith, and Fay roads
John Langenheim to prepare for special meeting/public hearing on June 22, 2026 for ZC2026-005 M3 text amendment
Trustee Hodges to look into gift certificates (e.g., Texas Roadhouse) for cleanup day volunteers
key_amounts: $500 anonymous donation to the fire department
Food gift cards from CP AAA donated to administration department (amount not specified)
notable_topics: New quint fire apparatus nearing completion at E-ONE plant in Florida, expected delivery in July
Medic unit in Columbus at Ford expected back in 2-3 weeks
Side mower delivery delayed to July 5th; renting skid steer attachment for interim roadside mowing
OPWC grant status upgraded to rep approved for paving Snyder, Smith, and Fay roads – tentative start second week of July
Objection letter to PUCO regarding Duke Energy natural gas distribution rate increases
Goshen Lions Club Flags for Heroes display approved for July and November
Special meeting scheduled June 22 for zoning text amendment public hearing (case ZC2026-005, M3)
New tablets installed for trustees at the dais
Cleanup day and Memorial Day activities recognized
Resignation of part-time firefighter/EMT Ryan DeAngelis
Clermont County Township Association also writing letters objecting to utility rate increases
notes: The meeting featured new electronic tablets for trustees at the dais, which was well-received. The executive session lasted approximately 43 minutes (6:14–6:57 p.m.). Travel costs for the quint inspection trip to E-ONE in Ocala, FL will be covered by E-ONE. The Clermont County Township Association is coordinating with individual townships to send objection letters to PUCO regarding rate increases.
title: BOT 6-9-26
